scope: Reference-driven GUI/UX parity criteria for selectable product-style presets. The criteria track independent visual/workflow resemblance without using proprietary assets or user-specific data.

reference_basis: User-approved connected-session MobaXterm-style reference screenshot with ribbon, quick connect, vertical rail, docked SFTP browser, remote monitoring, SSH banner, terminal and bottom telemetry.
User-approved MobaXterm-style home reference screenshot with centered welcome title, primary/recover actions, session search, recent sessions and footer notice.

reference_views.label: MobaXterm-style home reference
reference_views.basis: User-approved sanitized home reference view with welcome surface, quick actions, session search, recent sessions and footer notice.

reference_policy.note: Reference view uses project-owned synthetic labels and independent UI implementation; no copied assets, credentials or user-specific identifiers are stored.

reference_views.label: MobaXterm-style connected SSH/SFTP reference
reference_views.basis: User-approved sanitized connected-session reference view with ribbon, quick connect, SFTP dock, monitoring footer, SSH banner, terminal and telemetry.

reference_policy.note: Reference view uses project-owned synthetic labels and independent UI implementation; no copied assets, credentials or user-specific identifiers are stored.

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style top titlebar uses shared static/live metadata for connected-session title text, generated app mark, compact window controls and titlebar geometry without copying proprietary assets.

MobaXterm-style top chrome stack pins the titlebar, menu row, ribbon, Quick Connect strip, connected tab strip, terminal content start and left-dock offsets to one shared static/live geometry contract.
MobaXterm-style top menu row uses shared static/live metadata for Terminal, Sessions, View, X server, Tools, Games, Settings, Macros and Help, with stable keys and primary actions.
MobaXterm-style top menu row pins each menu label x offset, width, y offset, font size and gap through shared static/live geometry metadata.
Top menu and large ribbon actions cover session, server, tools, games, split, multiexec, tunneling, packages, settings, help, X server and exit affordances.
MobaXterm-style far-right ribbon edge actions tie the X server and Exit buttons to stable route metadata, generated icons, toolbar placement and live workflow handlers.

Static and live MobaXterm-style ribbons use independent drawn pictograms for session, servers, tools, games, sessions, split, multiexec, tunneling, packages, settings, help, X server and exit instead of letter placeholders or copied product assets.

scripts/check_real_gui_render.py: must use a generated ribbon icon

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style top ribbon pins each main and right-edge action width, icon offset, label offset, separator span and active-outline bounds through shared static/live geometry metadata.
Left chrome includes quick connect plus a role-based vertical icon rail for collapse, sessions, favorites, tools, macros and SFTP.
MobaXterm-style Quick Connect top strip uses shared metadata for placeholder text, dropdown marker, fixed height and focused state across static and live evidence.
MobaXterm-style Quick Connect suggestion dropdown uses shared metadata for typed query, saved-profile match, direct SSH fallback, row density and stable live item data.

Connected MobaXterm-style reference keeps Quick Connect idle by default with placeholder text and hidden suggestions so the SFTP/monitoring dock is immediately visible; typed query suggestion evidence remains separate.

scripts/check_real_gui_render.py: connected quick-connect suggestions must stay hidden

requirements.description: Connected MobaXterm-style vertical rail includes rotated section labels for Sessions, Tools, Macros and SFTP from shared metadata, matching the reference rail structure without copied assets.

MobaXterm-style left rail geometry pins rail width, icon x/y, static and live icon sizes, button bounds, active highlight bounds, rotated label height and generated-icon source through shared metadata.
MobaXterm-style saved user sessions keep the compact user-session tree while connected sessions can switch to SFTP.

MobaXterm-style saved-session tree geometry pins the user-session header, indentation, row heights, generated icon source, group/profile icon offsets, text offsets and selected-row bounds through shared static/live metadata.
MobaXterm-style density and spacing keep the compact dark shell, wide connected dock, narrow rail and tight tab/tree spacing without user-specific samples.

Static visual metrics pin screenshot-derived MobaXterm-style connected-session boundaries for the quick-connect strip, ribbon baseline, connected dock divider, tab baseline, SFTP dock bottom rule, telemetry divider and status bar.
Static visual metrics require the connected MobaXterm-style ribbon, dock, SFTP area, terminal and telemetry zones to keep their reference order and containment.
Connected-session reference uses a wide left SFTP/monitoring dock instead of a profile-tree plus duplicate in-workspace file pane.

Connected MobaXterm-style left dock frame uses shared side width, rail width, dock origin, dock size, workspace boundary, Quick Connect y/height and status y metadata across static preview, live PyQt widgets and real-render checks.
Live MobaXterm-style tabs switch the left dock between saved sessions and the active connected-session SFTP/monitoring view.
Connected MobaXterm-style tab strip uses shared home, inactive SSH session, active SSH session and plus-tab metadata with generated tab icons, close affordances and live tab role properties.
Connected MobaXterm-style tab strip pins home, inactive SSH, active SSH and plus-tab widths plus icon, label, close and gap offsets through shared static/live geometry metadata.

Connected MobaXterm-style session edge shortcut cluster tracks the small paperclip/settings controls near the tab and terminal edge with shared action metadata, generated icons and live widget properties.
Connected MobaXterm-style session edge shortcut icons pin static y, relative y, icon size, button size and generated-icon source through shared static/live placement metadata.
Connected MobaXterm-style session edge shortcut actions tie the attachment and settings buttons to stable workflow route metadata, generated icons, tab-strip placement and live handlers.
Connected MobaXterm-style terminal workspace has a narrow right utility rail with generated clip, settings and tool icons plus shared static/live stack geometry instead of text placeholders.
Connected MobaXterm-style right utility rail pins the rail width, live width, margins, action spacing and session-edge shortcut icon geometry through shared static/live chrome metadata.
Connected MobaXterm-style right utility rail actions tie the clip, settings and tools buttons to stable workflow route metadata, generated icons and live handlers.
SFTP browser shows toolbar actions, remote path, a selected parent-folder row, file name, size and last-modified columns.

MobaXterm-style SFTP browser path strip and file-table headers use shared static/live metadata for the remote path placeholder, dropdown marker, selected parent-folder row and keyed Name, Size and Last modified columns.
MobaXterm-style SFTP path strip, dropdown marker, table header labels and file-row text/icon offsets are pinned through shared static/live geometry metadata.

MobaXterm-style SFTP Follow terminal folder workflow ties the checked follow-folder control, active remote path field, follow SFTP list plan and file table state through shared static/live route metadata.

MobaXterm-style terminal-folder route proves the terminal working directory, Follow terminal folder checkbox, SFTP path field, selected parent row and SFTP list command are synchronized as one connected-session workflow.
MobaXterm-style SFTP file rows carry row-level follow-folder route evidence, active remote path metadata, selected parent-row state and stable row indexes instead of being generic file-list rows.
MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock uses independent drawn toolbar, generated file/folder/parent-row and monitoring glyphs instead of bracketed text placeholders in static and live evidence.

MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock uses shared toolbar action metadata, remote path strip, file-table columns, monitoring metric keys and follow-terminal-folder state across static and live render evidence.

MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock uses shared density metadata for the toolbar, path row, table header, file rows, monitoring panel and divider so static and live evidence keep the reference-like compact left-dock spacing.
MobaXterm-style SFTP toolbar actions are grouped into navigation, transfer, manage, mode and terminal clusters with reference-like separator boundaries across static and live evidence.
MobaXterm-style SFTP toolbar pins each connected-dock action button, icon and separator offset through shared static/live geometry metadata.

MobaXterm-style SFTP toolbar action route proves each connected-dock icon maps to file-transfer workflow metadata, generated icon identity, action grouping, target SFTP path/table objects and a live handler.
Remote monitoring is visible at the bottom of the left dock and is backed by SSH telemetry state.

MobaXterm-style connected left dock keeps the visible remote-monitoring area compact like the reference while retaining telemetry metric keys, refresh cadence, SSH command evidence, follow-folder SFTP plan metadata and shared control geometry for the bottom telemetry/status surface.

MobaXterm-style remote monitoring keeps the dock compact while explicitly routing CPU, memory, disk, network, connection and process evidence into the bottom telemetry bar through shared static/live metadata.

MobaXterm-style Remote monitoring control route proves the live toggle exposes checked state, SSH telemetry command, refresh cadence and bottom telemetry route evidence instead of only passive panel chrome.

MobaXterm-style Follow terminal folder control route proves the visible checkbox exposes checked state, active remote path, SFTP list plan and target SFTP browser/table linkage as first-class static/live evidence.

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style monitoring footer controls prove Remote monitoring and Follow terminal folder are live toggles wired through stable signals, handlers and runtime checked/path/plan properties.

MobaXterm-style remote-monitoring footer pins the divider span, footer height, content-left offset, metric gap and live controls frame width so the lower-left monitoring/follow-folder band stays reference-like in static and live evidence.

MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock exposes shared keyed controls for Remote monitoring and Follow terminal folder, with checked state, icon keys and tooltips validated in both static and live evidence.

MobaXterm-style monitoring footer pins the Remote monitoring and Follow terminal folder control baselines, font sizes, checkbox offset, checkmark stroke points and live widget widths through shared static/live metadata.
Follow-terminal-folder control is represented in the UI and maps to the SFTP list plan for the active path.
Connected SSH session banner shows direct SSH, compression, SSH-browser and X11 state.
MobaXterm-style SSH banner chrome uses shared centered title/subtitle and static/live geometry metadata so the generated preview and live PyQt banner keep the same compact terminal placement.

MobaXterm-style SSH banner capability card exposes the target line, keyed Direct SSH, SSH compression, SSH-browser and X11-forwarding rows, row status metadata and help/website footer links across static and live evidence.
MobaXterm-style SSH banner pins title, subtitle, target, capability and footer row positions plus the live banner slot top/left/gap through shared static/live row geometry metadata.

MobaXterm-style connected terminal transcript uses shared SSH/SFTP state for the web-console line, last-login line, prompt commands and health output across static preview and live PyQt terminal evidence.
MobaXterm-style connected terminal hides generic terminal controls and pins transcript row x/y, cadence and font-size metadata across static preview and live PyQt evidence.

Bottom connected-session status strip tracks target, CPU, RAM, disk, network, connection and process state as ordered reference-like cells with stable display text, icon keys, widths and separators across static and live evidence.

scripts/check_real_gui_render.py: must not be a text placeholder

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style bottom telemetry strip pins each cell start, height, icon offset, label offset, font size and separator span through shared static/live geometry metadata.

Connected MobaXterm-style chrome uses target-centric window/tab labels and icon-backed telemetry segments from shared SSH/SFTP state, using generic example targets instead of user-specific sample data.

scripts/check_real_gui_render.py: window title must be connected target label

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style active connected-session route ties the selected SSH tab to the SFTP dock, SFTP path/table, SSH banner, terminal transcript and bottom telemetry identity through shared static/live metadata.
MobaXterm-style connected session action route proves the active SSH tab exposes stable context/session action keys, labels and enabled-state capture through the live tab menu path.
MobaXterm-style connected session action menu route proves the live right-click menu is buildable, named and populated with actual QAction metadata matching the active SSH tab action route.

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style connected-session identity route keeps the window title, active tab label, SSH banner target, web console line, terminal prompt and bottom telemetry target synchronized through shared static/live metadata.
MobaXterm-style window status bar exposes SFTP, CPU monitoring and SSH-browser workflow state.

MobaXterm-style bottom status bar uses shared project-owned notice text, product note, keyed workflow segments and a compact right marker across static and live evidence without copying proprietary wording.
MobaXterm-style bottom edition/status footer pins notice, product-note, segment-start and right-marker coordinates through shared static/live geometry metadata.
MobaXterm-style bottom status edge includes compact previous-tab, next-tab and close-active controls from shared metadata, with generated arrows and close glyphs in static and live evidence.

MobaXterm-style home tab exposes a centered welcome surface with generated logo, primary/recover actions, session search, recent sessions and footer from shared metadata in both live GUI evidence and a dedicated static state preview rather than generic workflow cards.
MobaXterm-style home welcome geometry pins centered surface margins, hero spacing, logo/title/action/search/recent/footer offsets and live widget sizes through shared metadata.
MobaXterm-style preset-selection route proves the selected design combo value drives the toolbar, sidebar header, home tab label, status segments and Moba workspace evidence for the active preset.

MobaXterm-style visual signature keeps preset palette, density, tab behavior, toolbar icon size and pane/sidebar sizing synchronized between shared metadata, static previews and the live PyQt surface.
MobaXterm-style preset isolation route proves active product-specific chrome is visible while inactive product-specific widgets are hidden or absent after switching styles.
MobaXterm-style preset transition route proves switching from another selectable style to MobaXterm-style resets inactive product chrome from the previous style.
MobaXterm-style keyboard shortcut route proves the product preset exposes tracked shortcut keys, key sequences, action labels and live QShortcut bindings.
MobaXterm-style command surface route proves the product preset exposes visible command action order, labels, tooltips and active/checked/disabled state from shared static/live metadata.

MobaXterm-style focus interaction route proves the product preset exposes focused control, active toolbar, checked toolbar, disabled toolbar and selected tree-row interaction evidence from shared static/live metadata.
MobaXterm-style home search route proves the product preset exposes Home search, quick-connect/filter entry, Home actions and recent labels from shared static/live metadata.
MobaXterm-style preset catalog route proves the design selector exposes the complete ordered catalog, default Native option and product-style subset from shared static/live metadata.
MobaXterm-style interaction state tracks quick-connect focus, checked SFTP rail state and disabled sample ribbon affordance without user-specific data.
